headline: INTC Q2 blowout: rev +25% to $16.13B (fastest since 2011), DCAI +59% YoY to $6.3B — stock +13% AH; guides Q3 above street
summary: Intel reported Q2 revenue $16.13B (+25% YoY) vs $14.42B street, adj EPS $0.42 vs $0.21 est. Data Center & AI segment +59% YoY to $6.3B — management said DCAI 'cannot keep up with orders' and has signed 10 long-term fixed-price/volume contracts. Q3 guide $15.8-16.8B tops consensus. Stock +13% after-hours — validates the AI server CPU cycle and thickens competition for AMD EPYC.

headline: Trump forced-labor tariffs (10-12.5% on 60 countries) took effect 12:01 AM ET Friday — Taiwan, Korea, Japan, EU all hit
summary: Section 301 duties on ~60 trading partners kicked in overnight, backfilling the Section 122 blanket tariff that sunset. Mexico, UK, Canada, India face 10%; EU, Taiwan, Japan, South Korea, Switzerland face 10-12.5%. Direct exposure to AI hardware supply chain: TSM (Taiwan), 005930.KS/000660.KS (Korea), 8035.T/4063.T (Japan), ASML/IFX.DE/ENR.DE (EU). Section 232 semi duties unaffected. Watch for pass-through cost inflation on servers, memory, and lithography tools.

headline: TSLA closed -14.5% Thursday (worst intraday in >1yr): Q2 miss + AI/Cybercab/Optimus opex surge; FCF flipped to -$1.09B
summary: Follow-through from the Q2 miss: adj EPS $0.33 vs $0.50 est (miss 34%), revenue $28.24B (+25% YoY, beat). Opex +47% to $4.35B on AI, Cybercab, Optimus, Semi R&D. Op margin collapsed to 1.4% from 4.1%. Free cash flow -$1.09B vs +$1.44B prior quarter. Multiple sell-side desks trimmed PTs citing margin pressure and cautious autonomy guidance.

headline: NOW closed -17.8% Wednesday after Q2 beat — sharp derating despite AI ACV >$1B; enterprise gen-AI monetization skepticism setting in
summary: ServiceNow beat Q2 (EPS $0.90 vs $0.76) and raised FY subs guide, but the print couldn't hold the multiple: shares closed -17.8% at $84.78 as investors questioned whether Pro Plus AI SKU uptake is really translating into growth. Read-across risk to CRM, SNOW, PLTR — the market is punishing generative-AI-adjacent enterprise software that can't show a step-function in NRR or seats.

headline: Baird initiates CRWV at Outperform, PT $100; also starts Nebius at Outperform PT $250 — call is neocloud oversold vs. execution
summary: Baird's David Koning launched coverage of CoreWeave (CRWV) with Outperform / $100 PT, citing 'leadership in AI infrastructure, capacity execution, and NVIDIA relationship,' and initiated Nebius (NBIS) Outperform / $250. Comes after both names sold off ~25-30% in past month on Meta-Google $10B cloud deal read-across. Reinforces the thesis that CRWV's $99.4B backlog (incl. $21B Meta commitment) underpins revenue visibility through 2027.

headline: Meta in early talks to lease up to $10B of compute to Anthropic over 2 years — unusual dynamic: rival becomes infra supplier
summary: NYT/CNBC report Meta is negotiating to sell compute capacity to Anthropic in monthly installments over 2 years, up to ~$10B total, with mutual early-exit. Would help Meta monetize its aggressive AI capex build and compete directly with CoreWeave and Nebius. Notable because Meta's Llama competes head-on with Claude — signals hyperscalers pivoting toward selling excess capacity as their own build-outs outrun internal demand.

headline: VRT sell-side re-rack ahead of Jul 29 print: TD Cowen $387 (from $347) Buy; Oppenheimer $353 (from $330); RBC trims to $418 (from $435 Street-high)
summary: Analysts jockeying position ahead of Vertiv's Q2 report (July 29 BMO, consensus EPS ~$1.43). TD Cowen (Vitanza) raised to $387, Oppenheimer (Kaye) $353, Baird initiated Outperform $370, KeyBanc Overweight $360. RBC still bull-case leader at $418 but trimmed from $435. Stock up 107% YTD on Q1 orders +252% YoY and $15B backlog — liquid cooling is the direct GB200/GB300 read-through.

headline: KeyBanc utilities re-rank: DUK and AEE upgraded to Overweight (targets $139, $122), SO cut to Underweight — datacenter load thesis narrows
summary: KeyBanc rotated regulated utilities Thursday: Duke Energy (DUK) and Ameren (AEE) upgraded to Overweight, Southern Co (SO) cut to Underweight. Reads as a narrowing of the 'datacenter load growth' trade — analysts increasingly distinguishing utilities with dedicated hyperscaler contracts / co-located generation from those pricing in speculative load. Positive read-through for CEG, VST, NEE which have signed 20-year AI PPAs; watch AES which is more diversified.

headline: Brent tops $100 on Red Sea tanker strikes; 10Y yield at YTD high — macro headwind stacks on AI capex anxiety into Friday open
summary: Two Saudi oil tankers reportedly struck in the Red Sea sent Brent above $100 for the first time since May. The 10-year Treasury yield hit its highest level since early 2025 as traders reprice a Fed hike into the next 2-3 meetings. S&P 500 closed -1.21% Thursday (worst since June 23), Nasdaq 100 -1.9%. Polymarket implies 66% chance of an S&P open-up on Friday, but the setup — oil shock + tariff step-up + rate-hike odds + GOOGL/TSLA/NOW capex/spend anxiety — is fragile.
